subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the narrative structure of the poem
Interpret symbolism and imagery used in the poem
Evaluate character motivations and ethical decisions
Compare the values represented by Gopi and the Zamindar
Apply the moral lessons of the story to modern society
Develop analytical reading, creative thinking, and reflective writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Fantasy and Symbolism
teaching_strategy: Examples of symbolic objects in stories
Guided reading of the poem’s opening lines
student_activities: Discuss how magical objects represent deeper ideas
Write a short reflection on what the magic brush might symbolize

lesson_plan.title: Character Analysis – Gopi
topics_covered: Role of compassion in the story
student_activities: Identify actions that reveal Gopi’s character
Write a character sketch of Gopi

lesson_plan.title: Conflict and Social Commentary
topics_covered: Symbolism of greed and misuse of power
teaching_strategy: Discussion on social inequality and power
student_activities: Create a comparison chart of Gopi and the Zamindar
Discuss how greed can create conflict in society

lesson_plan.title: Climax and Creative Problem Solving
topics_covered: Symbolism of the road, horse, and river
teaching_strategy: Close reading of the climax scene
student_activities: Explain how each painted object helped Gopi escape
Design their own creative solution to a problem using imagination

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Moral Interpretation
topics_covered: Relevance of the poem’s message today
student_activities: Debate: Should powerful abilities always be used for others?
Write a reflective paragraph connecting the story to modern life

lesson_plan.title: Literary Devices and Creative Extension
student_activities: Identify examples of imagery and repetition in the poem
Write an alternative ending to the story

teaching_aids: Illustrations of scenes from the poem
Worksheets for symbolism and literary devices
teacher_tip: Encourage students to interpret the magic brush symbolically and discuss how creativity, when used responsibly, can create positive change in society.
